# Simple Liturgy

> A focused, offline-ready web reader for the 1979 Book of Common Prayer Daily Office lectionary and daily prayers.

## Primary site

- [Simple Liturgy](https://simpleliturgy.com/): Open the reader and installable web app.

## What the app provides

- Daily Office citations for Psalms, Old Testament, New Testament, and Gospel readings.
- A prayer-first focus sequence followed by the readings and Gloria.
- Keyboard, touch, and swipe navigation with responsive portrait and landscape layouts.
- A citation companion meant to be paired with a physical Bible; the app does not reproduce Scripture text.
- Offline access after the web app has been fully loaded or installed once while online.

## Offline behavior

- The app saves its interface, lectionary citations, and prayers for airplane-mode use.
- The first complete load or installation requires an internet connection.
- Browsers may clear stored site data under storage pressure, and reconnecting is required to receive updates.

## Project background

- Simple Liturgy is a Beginner's Apprenticeship project.
- [Beginner's Apprenticeship](https://beginnersapprenticeship.com/about) creates inter-denominational books, music, podcasts, and simple liturgies that adapt historic Christian prayer rhythms for families and households with small children.

## Reference and source

- [BCP Online Daily Office Lectionary](https://www.bcponline.org/DOLectionary/dolectionary.html): Reference used for calendar and citation review.
- [Simple Liturgy source](https://github.com/parksmithh/simple-liturgy): Production website source.
- [Daily Office Reader staging source](https://github.com/parksmithh/daily-office-reader): Hardware, firmware, data, and staging web app development.
- [Terms & Licensing](https://simpleliturgy.com/terms.html): Personal-use, contribution, trademark, and commercial-permission terms.
- [Source license](https://simpleliturgy.com/LICENSE.md): PolyForm Noncommercial License 1.0.0 for covered web software.
- [License notice](https://simpleliturgy.com/NOTICE): Copyright notice and repository scope exclusions.
- [Contributing terms](https://simpleliturgy.com/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contributor rights and grant terms.

Simple Liturgy is an independent project.
